expectations.text: The review identifies the test file importing from '../src/internal/helpers' as a test boundary violation
expectations.evidence: Report [Important] tests/user-service.test.ts:2: 'Tests import internals directly, coupling tests to implementation details... bypasses package public boundary.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the comment 'Used by the UserRegistration component in pages/register.tsx and the AdminPanel' as upstream knowledge that will rot
expectations.evidence: Refactoring Changes: 'Removed transient/knowledge-direction comments and implementation-noise comments to keep comments focused on durable code intent.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the 'export * from ./internal/helpers' in index.ts as leaking internal implementation details through the barrel
expectations.evidence: Report [Minor] src/index.ts:3: 'Public API surface expanded to include internal helpers via wildcard export... makes internal implementation utilities part of external contract.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the constructor doing require('pg') and new Client() as a DI anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Report [Important] src/user-service.ts:29-35: 'Domain service now directly depends on Postgres client (pg) and connection setup... couples business logic to infrastructure. Recommendation: Move audit persistence behind an injected interface.'

expectations.text: The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)
expectations.evidence: Report uses **[Important]** and **[Minor]** with file:line, Evidence, Recommendation throughout.
